page: Amazon Web Services r6i.4xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 16 virtual CPUs across 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The dedicated CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz and includes a 54 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Benchmark results place the r6i.4xlarge in the average performance tier across multiple categories. In CPU-focused tests, it achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1634.0 and a multi-core score of 9766.0, alongside a Passmark CPU score of 21093.88. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 828.29 GB/sec and an uncached large block read bandwidth of 96.3 GB/sec. For specialized workloads, the instance processes Redis SET operations at 15.38 million operations per second and handles static web serving at a throughput of 21.16 GB/sec. Small-scale LLM inference using a 135M model yields 1054.11 tokens per second for prompt processing, though performance drops to 32.47 tokens per second for a medium 7B model.

From a resource density perspective, the r6i.4xlarge offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8:1, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. The lack of bundled local storage means all storage operations must rely on network-attached volumes, which represents a tradeoff for disk-intensive tasks. Given its hardware profile and benchmark performance, including a Passmark database operations score of 7768.55, this instance is configured for memory-intensive applications, in-memory databases like Redis, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity.
description: Amazon Web Services r6i.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 16 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and a 3.5 GHz clock speed. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory, establishing an 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data indicates average-tier performance, with a Passmark CPU score of 21093.88, a Redis SET throughput of 15.38 million operations per second, and a cached small-block memory read bandwidth of 828.29 GB/sec. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive workloads, such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and data processing tasks that do not require local NVMe storage or hardware acceleration.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6i.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 128 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 3.5 GHz processor, designed for memory-intensive workloads without local storage.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated Intel Xeon processing for memory-bound workloads.

128.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing an 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio
6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU
Redis performance of 15.38 million SET operations per second
Suited for in-memory databases, caching, and memory-bound applications
